hero.question: Will Ubisoft be acquired before 2027?
hero.prob_take: At 38.5%, the crowd is pricing in roughly 2-in-5 odds of acquisition by 2027, down 10 points in 24 hours after what looks like profit-taking on recent activist noise. The real tell is that Ubisoft's operational struggles (Star Wars Outlaws missed, live-service failures) actually make them a less attractive acquisition target at current valuations, not more, which means this market may have gotten ahead of the narrative.

daily_take.headline: Ubisoft M&A Odds Collapse 10 Points. Smart Money Smells Blood.
daily_take.deck: Ubisoft's acquisition odds cratered to 38.5% overnight, shedding a full 10 percentage points in 24 hours. The market is pricing in either a failed deal environment or a signal that the French gaming giant's strategic value just got repriced downward. This smells like capitulation from deal believers, not rational valuation adjustment. Contrarian play: 38.5% is too cheap if you believe consolidation in gaming stays live through 2026.

sidebar.headline: Brazilian real volatility is pricing in either central bank hawkishness or BRL weakness that won't materialize. The 29-point jump to 36.5% suggests fresh conviction on the upside.
sidebar.label: USD/BRL 6.50: 36.5%

sidebar.headline: Vitality's Rotterdam odds gained 9.5 points on event momentum, but 47% still leaves room to move if the team's recent form extends into qualifiers.
sidebar.label: Vitality Rotterdam Win: 47.0%

sidebar.headline: Anduril's IPO odds got torched 22 points. Either the defense tech market just cooled or this was overpriced at 43.5%. Current 21.5% might be the real bottom.
sidebar.label: Anduril IPO Pre-2027: 21.5%
